nodes.context: Canadian organized crime group founded in 2010, a consortium of several groups.
Canadian outlaw motorcycle club, allowed members to create side projects like the Wolfpack.
Canadian organized crime group, leader Jonathan Bacon was a co-founder of the Wolfpack.
Canadian organized crime group, leaders Randy Naicker and James Riach were co-founders of the Wolfpack.
Mexican drug cartel, supplied cocaine to the Wolfpack.
Canadian organized crime family, members joined the Wolfpack; Nabil Alkhalil served as liaison with Sinaloa Cartel.
Montreal-based organized crime group, controlled Port of Montreal; member Shane Maloney was a Wolfpack leader.
Montreal Mafia family; Martino Caputo served as their agent for the greater Toronto area.
Vancouver-based organized crime group, rivals of the Wolfpack; co-bosses Gurmit Singh Dhak and Sandip Duhre.
Canadian organized crime group, aligned with Dhak-Duhre group against the Wolfpack.
Organized crime group listed as rival in infobox.
Canadian organized crime group, a consortium of several groups, active in drug trafficking.
Mexican drug cartel that used the Wolfpack to establish a distribution network in Canada.
Traditional 'Ndrangheta family from Siderno, Italy, involved in a gang war with the Wolfpack.
Canadian organized crime group mentioned as still active alongside the Wolfpack.
Canadian organized crime group mentioned as still active alongside the Wolfpack.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack Alliance was founded in 2010 in the Lower Mainland of British Columbia by the Hells Angels member Larry Amero as a 'side project'.
edges.context: Hells Angels member Larry Amero founded the Wolfpack as a side project, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: The other leaders of the Wolfpack Alliance were Jonathan Bacon of the Red Scorpions
edges.context: Red Scorpions leader Jonathan Bacon was a co-founder of the Wolfpack.

edges.evidence_quote: The other leaders of the Wolfpack Alliance were ... Randy Naicker and James Riach of the Independent Soldiers
edges.context: Independent Soldiers leaders were co-founders of the Wolfpack.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack worked as distributors of cocaine from the Sinaloa Cartel of Mexico
edges.context: Wolfpack distributed cocaine supplied by the Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack soon took in other members, most notably the Alkhalil family
edges.context: Alkhalil family members joined the Wolfpack, with Nabil serving as liaison to Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: A key member of the Wolfpack proved to be Shane Kenneth 'Wheels' Maloney of the West End Gang.
edges.context: West End Gang member Shane Maloney was a key Wolfpack leader, and the West End Gang controlled the Port of Montreal used for smuggling.

edges.evidence_quote: Caputo, who officially worked as a property developer ... served as the Rizzuto family's agent for the greater Toronto area.
edges.context: Wolfpack member Martino Caputo acted as agent for the Rizzuto family.

edges.evidence_quote: Suhhveer 'Sukh' Dhak, the younger brother of Gurmit, believed that the Wolfpack Alliance were the ones responsible for his brother's murder, and vowed to avenge him.
edges.context: Wolfpack was in a violent conflict with the Dhak-Duhre group, with murders on both sides.

edges.evidence_quote: The United Nations gang aligned itself with the Dhak-Duhre group against the Wolfpack.
edges.context: United Nations gang sided with Dhak-Duhre group against the Wolfpack, indicating conflict.
Infobox lists Siderno Group as a rival.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack were used by Guzmán and the Sinaloa Cartel to establish a drug distribution network in Canada, which allowed them to ultimately by-pass the Wolfpack, as the Sinaloa cartel took over the networks that the Wolfpack had established.
edges.context: The Sinaloa Cartel used the Wolfpack as middlemen for drug distribution, then bypassed them.

edges.evidence_quote: The Sinaloa Cartel had long had doubts about the competence of the Wolfpack, especially Nero, and decided after his arrest to move into Canada themselves.
edges.context: The Sinaloa Cartel lost confidence in the Wolfpack and began operating independently in Canada.

edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack became involved in a gang war in Toronto in 2016-2018 with the Siderno Group.
edges.context: The Wolfpack and Siderno Group were engaged in a gang war in Toronto from 2016 to 2018.

edges.evidence_quote: Lindsey Houghton of CFSEU-BC stated: 'We’ve seen and heard the name Wolfpack over the last year . We’ve heard their name come up in places like Kamloops and Surrey. So they are still very active and groups like the Red Scorpions and Independent Soldiers – these groups are still active and prominent in that world.'
edges.context: The Wolfpack, Red Scorpions, and Independent Soldiers are all active organized crime groups in British Columbia.

edges.evidence_quote: Lindsey Houghton of CFSEU-BC stated: 'We’ve seen and heard the name Wolfpack over the last year . We’ve heard their name come up in places like Kamloops and Surrey. So they are still very active and groups like the Red Scorpions and Independent Soldiers – these groups are still active and prominent in that world.'
edges.context: The Wolfpack, Red Scorpions, and Independent Soldiers are all active organized crime groups in British Columbia.
